name: Cozy Cats Fit
short_description: A fun cat puzzle game, with music and adorable cats keeping you company.
detailed_description:
Welcome to Cozy Cats Fit !

This is a cat-themed puzzle game. Just place all the cats into the grid to win!

  • Classic Polyomino gameplay rules

  • 28 adorable cats to keep you company

  • 100 carefully selected challenge levels

  • Hundreds of random challenge levels

Enjoy hours of relaxing fun.

supported_languages: English, French, German, Spanish - Spain, Russian, Japanese, Simplified Chinese, Traditional Chinese, Portuguese - Brazil, Korean

reviews.review_text:
[H1]When your puzzle pieces have eyes and jiggle physics 😻[/H1]

I never thought I’d reach a point in life where I’d casually ask for jiggle physics in a cat tetromino puzzle. And yet, here we are. If you’ve played [url=https://store.steampowered.com/app/1369340/Cats_Organized_Neatly/?curator_clanid=45639970]Cats Organized Neatly[/url], [url=https://store.steampowered.com/app/2810330/Cats_in_Boxes/?curator_clanid=45639970]Cats in Boxes[/url] or [url=https://store.steampowered.com/app/4390700/NeNeKoNeKo/?curator_clanid=45639970]NeNeKoNeKo[/url], you already know the deal: place all the cats into a grid and make it work. This one just adds extra fluff, extra wiggle, and a whole lot of big, watchful kitty eyes following your every move.

The loop is pretty simple: 100 premade puzzles for about 2h30 of gameplay, then a steady stream of randomly generated ones if you still crave more. There’s a quick reset button for when things go sideways, and the overall difficulty stays very approachable. It’s more about relaxing and arranging than actual challenges, which fits the cozy vibe perfectly. Presentation is the main point here. The cats are adorable and so soft-looking! Plus there’s a full cat almanac with 28 little companions to collect. You can swap between different soundtracks, mute sound and music, pick from four gentle background colors, and go fullscreen for maximum cat immersion. It all feels very comfy and easy to settle into.

Achievements are straightforward and follow normal progression, which is always appreciated. That said, one of them goes full marathon mode: completing 300 grids. Since puzzles turn random after the first 100, there’s no way to track your progress, so you’re just trusting the process. It’s less fun goal and more “hope you like doing this a lot”. Also, one small note from the store page: some audio resources were generated using AI. It doesn’t impact the gameplay itself, but it’s worth knowing depending on your preferences.

All in all, this is a fluffy, feel-good puzzle you can sink a couple of hours into without stress. It may not be the most challenging entry in the genre, especially with other similar titles around, but it absolutely delivers a cozy, wiggly little cat session when you’re in the mood for something light.

[quote]For more reviews like this one, please check out [url=https://store.steampowered.com/curator/45639970-Ele%2527s-Little-Recommendation-Corner/][b]Ele's Little Recommendation Corner[/b][/url]![/quote]
